title: Automated Client Reporting for Consulting Firms
metaDescription: Weekly and monthly client updates that draft themselves from live calendar, notes, and time data. The partner reviews and sends. Done-for-you reporting, not a template.
excerpt: Weekly status updates and monthly engagement reports usually take partner time on a Friday. They do not have to. The data is already in your calendar and notes; the update should draft itself.

sections.intro:
Consulting firms ship two kinds of reporting work. Weekly status updates to each active engagement, and monthly or end-of-phase reports for the bigger pieces. Both usually live on a partner's Friday afternoon. Both are mostly summarization, not new thinking.

The inputs are already there: meeting notes, calendar entries, time tracker data, the Slack channel for that engagement. The update should draft itself, in your firm's voice, and land on the partner's desk ready to review and send.

This is the Cadence layer of an AI Operating System. We install it after the Lead Engine for most firms.
sections.fourCFit:
Reporting is a Cadence capability: it runs on a schedule without anyone asking. It depends on:

  • Connections: your calendar, notes, time tracker, and engagement-specific Slack channels.
  • Context: your firm's voice, the structure of your standard status update, and the goals for each engagement.
  • Capabilities: the draft itself, formatted to whatever document or doc-tool you ship today.

See it in context at AI Operating System for Consulting Firms.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
• Weekly status drafts on Friday morning per engagement, automatically.
  • Pulls from this week's meetings, notes, decisions, and time entries.
  • Written in your firm's voice, structured exactly the way your firm structures status (decisions made, risks, next week, asks).
  • Format matches your existing document (Google Doc, Notion, slide, email body).
  • Partner reviews, edits if needed, sends. Time per update drops from 30 to 60 minutes to under 10.
  • Monthly or end-of-phase reports use the same engine with a longer horizon.
sections.howWeInstall:
Three phases:

  1. Audit, free, about 10 minutes. We confirm reporting fit and score readiness. Run the audit.
  2. Install, inside 10 business days for the reporting Capability. We connect your data sources, capture your firm's voice from past status updates, and ship the first draft.
  3. Operate, ongoing. We watch it, fix it, and extend per-engagement structure as part of your plan. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Time per status update: from 30 to 60 minutes to under 10.
  • Coverage: 100 percent of engagements reported on the same day, every week.
  • Consistency: every engagement gets the same structure and depth, regardless of which partner is responsible.

Standing guarantee on the wider AIOS engagement: recover 40 or more hours a month across the team, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
What if an engagement needs a custom structure?

Per-engagement structure is part of how the system is configured. The custom view is the default for that engagement, every week.

What about confidentiality?

Every connection and prompt lives in your accounts. Nothing is shared cross-engagement. The system operates exactly as a junior would, scoped to the data they are allowed to see.

Who owns the system?

You do. Every prompt and connection lives in your accounts.
